whenToUse: When calculating odd percentages like 37.5%, 62.5%, 87.5%
trick: Convert % to fraction: 37.5% = 3/8, so 37.5% of 240 = 3/8 × 240 = 90

example.question: Find 37.5% of 800

whenToUse: When two percentage changes occur one after another
trick: Net % = A + B + (A×B)/100. For increase use +, for decrease use -

example.question: Price increased by 20%, then decreased by 10%. Net change?

whenToUse: When SP and CP are given in ratio form
trick: Profit % = (SP - CP)/CP × 100 = (SP/CP - 1) × 100

example.question: CP:SP = 4:5. Find profit %

whenToUse: When false weight is used in transactions
trick: Profit % = (Error/Actual weight) × 100 where Error = False weight - True weight

example.question: Uses 900g as 1kg (false profit). CP = Rs.20/kg. SP = Rs.25/kg. Total profit %?

whenToUse: When three ratios a:b, b:c, c:d are given
trick: Make middle terms equal by multiplying, then combine all ratios

example.question: A:B = 2:3, B:C = 4:5, C:D = 6:7. Find A:B:C:D

whenToUse: When work is completed in fractions or parts
trick: If work done = a/b, then remaining work = 1 - a/b. Time remaining = (remaining work) ÷ work rate

example.question: A completes 1/3 work in 5 days. Total time for complete work?
example.solution: Work rate = (1/3)/5 = 1/15 per day. Total time = 1 ÷ (1/15) = 15 days

whenToUse: When two persons work on alternate days
trick: Work in 2 days = 1/a + 1/b. Total time = 2 × (total work ÷ work in 2 days)

example.question: A works day 1, B works day 2. A alone in 20 days, B in 30 days. Total time?
example.solution: Work in 2 days = 1/20 + 1/30 = 1/12. Total time = 2 × 1 ÷ (1/12) = 24 days

whenToUse: When two objects move towards or away from each other
trick: Towards each other: Time = Distance ÷ (Speed1 + Speed2). Same direction: Time = Distance ÷ |Speed1 - Speed2|

example.question: Two cars 100km apart, speeds 40 and 60 km/hr towards each other. Meeting time?

whenToUse: When train crosses a platform or another train
trick: Crossing time = (Train length + Platform length) ÷ Train speed. Convert speed to m/s if needed

example.question: Train 300m at 60km/hr crosses 200m platform. Time?

whenToUse: When compound interest and simple interest difference is asked for 2 years
trick: CI - SI = P × (R/100)² where P = Principal, R = Rate

example.question: P = Rs.10000, R = 10%, 2 years. Find CI - SI

whenToUse: When equal installments are paid on compound interest
trick: For amount A, rate r%, n installments: Each installment = A × r/100 ÷ [(1+r/100)^n - 1]

example.question: Loan Rs.12100 at 10% CI, 2 equal annual installments?

whenToUse: When same distance covered at different speeds
trick: Avg Speed = 2 × S1 × S2 ÷ (S1 + S2). Harmonic mean of speeds

example.question: Go at 40 km/hr, return at 60 km/hr. Average speed?

whenToUse: When one value is replaced by another in a set
trick: Change in average = (New value - Old value) ÷ Number of items

example.question: Average of 5 numbers = 20. Replace 15 with 35. New average?

whenToUse: When checking divisibility or solving complex number problems
trick: Keep adding digits until single digit. Digital root of product = product of digital roots

example.question: Find remainder when 789 × 456 is divided by 9
example.solution: Digital root of 789 = 6, of 456 = 6. Product digital root = 6×6 = 36 = 9. So remainder = 0

whenToUse: When finding unit digit of large powers

example.question: Find unit digit of 2^25
example.solution: 25 ÷ 4 = 6 remainder 1. First in cycle = 2. So unit digit = 2

whenToUse: When expressions involve x + 1/x and its powers
trick: If x + 1/x = k, then x² + 1/x² = k² - 2, x³ + 1/x³ = k³ - 3k

example.question: If x + 1/x = 3, find x³ + 1/x³

whenToUse: When finding max/min of quadratic expressions
trick: For ax² + bx + c: If a > 0, minimum at x = -b/2a. If a < 0, maximum at x = -b/2a

example.question: Find minimum value of x² + 4x + 5

whenToUse: When checking if triangle is right-angled

example.question: Is triangle with sides 15, 36, 39 right-angled?
example.solution: 15² + 36² = 225 + 1296 = 1521 = 39². Yes, it's a multiple of (5,12,13) triplet

whenToUse: When comparing areas of similar shapes
trick: Area ratio = (side ratio)² for similar figures. Volume ratio = (side ratio)³

example.question: Two squares sides ratio 2:3. Area ratio?

whenToUse: When angles add to 90°
trick: sin(90-°) = cos°, cos(90-°) = sin°, tan(90-°) = cot°, cosec(90-°) = sec°

example.question: Find sin 30° if cos 60° = 0.5

whenToUse: When trigonometric values of 15°, 75° are needed

example.question: Find tan 15°

whenToUse: When converting percentage to degrees in pie chart
trick: Degrees = Percentage × 3.6 (since 100% = 360°, so 1% = 3.6°)

example.question: Convert 25% to degrees in pie chart

whenToUse: When exact values not needed, only comparisons
trick: Use visual estimation and relative comparison rather than exact reading

example.question: Which month had highest sales? (Bar graph shows July tallest)
example.solution: July has tallest bar, so highest sales

whenToUse: When finding specific data in large tables
trick: Scan headers first, then look for key values, use finger to track rows/columns

example.question: Find sales of Product B in Q2 from 5×4 table
example.solution: Locate Product B row, Q2 column, read intersection value

whenToUse: When analyzing trends over time
trick: Look for overall pattern (increasing/decreasing), identify peaks and troughs, note rate of change

example.question: What is the trend from 2018-2022? (Line shows steady upward movement)
example.solution: Consistent increasing trend with no major fluctuations

whenToUse: When some data is missing but total is given
trick: Use total - sum of known values = missing value. Check for patterns if multiple missing

example.question: Total sales = 500. Jan=100, Feb=120, Mar=80, Apr=?
